name: Palanquins from Valencia de Don Juan - circular via Mansilla de las Mulas and Santas Martas
name_original: Palanquinos desde Valencia de Don Juan – circular por Mansilla de las Mulas y Santas Martas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Valencia De Don Juan traverse the fertile plains of the Esla river and the undulating landscapes of the Oteros region in León province, Spain. The area offers a mix of gentle slopes and open plains, providing varied terrain for road cyclists. Routes often feature extensive agricultural fields and riverside paths, with elevations typically remaining below 500 meters. Best road cycling routes around Valencia De Don Juan The most popular road cycling route is Mayorga and Valderas Castle Loop from Valencia de Don Juan, a 52.8 miles (84.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route offers a blend of rural views and passes by historical castles. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Palanquins from Valencia de Don Juan - circular via Mansilla de las Mulas and Santas Martas, a moderate 41.6 miles (67.0 km) path. This route winds through agricultural plains and offers views of the local countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas, a 65.1 miles (104.7 km) trail leading through the Esla river valley, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Valencia De Don Juan is defined by the Esla river valley, agricultural fields, and undulating plains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from gentle rides to longer, moderate routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valencia De Don Juan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Valencia de Don Juan?
A: Road cycling routes in Valencia de Don Juan primarily traverse the fertile plains of the Esla river and the undulating landscapes of the Oteros region. You can expect a mix of gentle slopes and open plains, with extensive agricultural fields and pleasant riverside paths. Elevations typically remain below 500 meters, offering varied terrain suitable for different cycling abilities.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Valencia de Don Juan?
A: Yes, there are options for beginners and families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdevimbre – Grain Silo in Palanquinos loop from Palanquinos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route. Additionally, the Vía Verde del Esla, an 11-kilometer converted railway line, offers a compacted surface and very low gradient, making it an ideal and enjoyable ride for all skill levels, including families.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of road cycling routes in this region?
A: The road cycling routes around Valencia de Don Juan generally range from easy to moderate. Our guide features one easy route and six moderate routes, ensuring there are options for both those seeking a relaxed ride and cyclists looking for a bit more challenge without extreme difficulty.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes in Valencia de Don Juan?
A: You will primarily encounter the rich natural beauty of the Esla river valley, characterized by extensive agricultural plains, orchards, and pastures. The routes also offer views of the 'comarca de los Oteros' and the 'Tierra de Campos' plains, providing a blend of natural and agricultural scenery. The Vía Verde del Esla is particularly good for observing local flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of historical landmarks. The 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/20149061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Coyanza&lt;/a&gt; (also known as Valencia de Don Juan Castle) is a prominent landmark perched above the Esla river valley, offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046763381/castillo-de-coyanza-from-gordoncillo-circular-via-castillo-de-altafria-and-valderas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas&lt;/a&gt; route passes by historical castles. The Church of Nuestra Señora del Castillo Viejo, next to the castle, also provides scenic views.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Valencia de Don Juan?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Valencia de Don Juan are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046812916/mayorga-and-valderas-castle-loop-from-valencia-de-don-juan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mayorga and Valderas Castle Loop from Valencia de Don Juan&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046819648/palanquins-from-valencia-de-don-juan-circular-via-mansilla-de-las-mulas-and-santas-martas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palanquins from Valencia de Don Juan - circular via Mansilla de las Mulas and Santas Martas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Valencia de Don Juan?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are mild. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winter offers cooler temperatures, but the relatively low elevation means routes are often accessible, though checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046763381/castillo-de-coyanza-from-gordoncillo-circular-via-castillo-de-altafria-and-valderas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 65.1-mile (104.7 km) trail. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046812916/mayorga-and-valderas-castle-loop-from-valencia-de-don-juan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mayorga and Valderas Castle Loop from Valencia de Don Juan&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 52.8 miles (84.9 km).

Q: Are there any specific routes that showcase the agricultural plains and riverside paths?
A: Many routes highlight these features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046819648/palanquins-from-valencia-de-don-juan-circular-via-mansilla-de-las-mulas-and-santas-martas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palanquins from Valencia de Don Juan - circular via Mansilla de las Mulas and Santas Martas&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, winds through agricultural plains and offers views of the local countryside. The banks of the Esla river also feature dedicated paths for pleasant rides alongside the water.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Valencia de Don Juan?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the region for its diverse and picturesque landscapes, particularly the blend of fertile river plains and gently undulating terrain. The extensive network of routes, including those passing by historical castles and through charming rural areas, is often highlighted. The area's suitability for various ability levels, from easy rides to more moderate challenges, is also a key draw.

Q: Can I find routes that offer a mix of rural views and historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046812916/mayorga-and-valderas-castle-loop-from-valencia-de-don-juan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mayorga and Valderas Castle Loop from Valencia de Don Juan&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a blend of rural views and passing by historical castles. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046763381/castillo-de-coyanza-from-gordoncillo-circular-via-castillo-de-altafria-and-valderas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas&lt;/a&gt; also takes you through the Esla river valley with historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: While the region is known for its gentle slopes, some routes offer longer distances and moderate elevation gains that can provide a good challenge for experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046763381/castillo-de-coyanza-from-gordoncillo-circular-via-castillo-de-altafria-and-valderas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Coyanza from Gordoncillo – circular via Castillo de Altafria and Valderas&lt;/a&gt;, at over 100 km with nearly 500 meters of ascent, is one of the more demanding options in the guide.
tips: The Castillo de Coyanza or Valencia de Don Juan is an extensive and beautiful fortified castle from the 11th century on the banks of the Esla River. The impressive castle and gardens can be seen from the road.

This small town, which shares a municipality with the neighboring Gigosos de los Oteros, is a quiet and pleasant place where you can perhaps make a different stop and try some wine or pomace in a private winery, as long as you don't have much more to pedal. ..
Ruins of fortifications and walls around the old town of Mansilla de las Mulas on the banks of the Esla river. The town is located on the Camino de Santiago Francés.
Small agricultural town on the N-601 national road surrounded by wheat fields.
